name: Hex Data Workspace and Analytics API
what_it_does: Collaborative data workspace with REST API — enabling data teams to write SQL and Python in notebook-style projects, build interactive data apps for business users, share analyses with automatic versioning, schedule refreshes, and collaborate with teammates in real-time, combining the flexibility of Jupyter notebooks with the shareability of dashboards.
use_cases: Triggering Hex project runs and refreshes via API for automated reporting pipelines
Managing Hex projects and workspaces via API for team workflow automation
Integrating Hex scheduled runs with data orchestration tools (dbt, Airflow) via API
Querying Hex project run status and output metadata via API for pipeline monitoring
Embedding Hex data apps in internal tools via API-generated embed links
not_for: Business user self-service BI without SQL knowledge (use Tableau or Power BI)
Production data pipelines (use dbt or Airflow for data transformation pipelines)
Real-time operational monitoring (use Grafana or Datadog for metrics)
best_when: A data team with SQL and Python expertise needs a collaborative notebook environment that also produces shareable, interactive data apps for business stakeholders — bridging the gap between data science notebooks and self-service dashboards.
avoid_when: Your business users need self-service BI without SQL, you need real-time dashboards, or your data team primarily uses R rather than Python/SQL.

auth.notes: API token from Hex workspace settings. Bearer token auth. Workspace-scoped access. No webhooks. REST API for project run management and workspace administration. dbt Cloud and Airflow integration for orchestration. Integration with Slack for run notifications. No OAuth2. No fine-grained scopes — API token provides workspace-level access.

pricing.notes: San Francisco company. Founded 2020. Raised $52M Series B (2022). Backed by Andreessen Horowitz (a16z), Redpoint Ventures. YC S21. Collaborative notebook + data app platform. Differentiator: real-time collaborative editing (like Google Docs for data notebooks). dbt integration for data lineage. SQL + Python in same notebook. Scheduled runs for automated reporting. Growing strongly in data-native companies (Square, Notion, Ramp use Hex). Magic AI (LLM-powered SQL generation) is recent addition.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per workspace; not publicly documented

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for project run management. API token bearer auth. Good documentation. No webhooks — polling for run status. dbt and Airflow integration for orchestration. Magic AI (LLM SQL generation) via API. Rate limits not documented publicly. US data residency only. a16z-backed company with startup security maturity.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Project reads fully idempotent. Run status reads idempotent by run ID. Run triggering is not idempotent — creates new run each call. Project creation not idempotent — check by name.
known_agent_gotchas: No webhooks — must poll for run completion status
Rate limits not documented — test before high-frequency pipeline orchestration
US data residency only — EU customers need to evaluate GDPR adequacy
Run triggering creates new run each time — implement idempotency at orchestration layer
API is primarily for run management, not project content creation (use UI for that)

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2 and GDPR compliant. US data residency. API token auth. a16z-backed company with enterprise security standards. Notebook data and database credentials stored in Hex — restrict access to authorized users. Magic AI (LLM) may send query context to AI provider — review data handling policy. No OAuth2 or fine-grained scopes. Database connection credentials must be secured in Hex secrets.
